title: API integrations for business systems | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)

seo.title: API integrations for business systems | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
seo.description: Where API integrations make sense, what matters beyond endpoint mapping, and how to avoid fragile system-to-system links with no operational safety.

page.title: API integrations for business systems | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: API integrations for business systems, workflows, and portals
page.description: Where API integrations make sense, what matters beyond endpoint mapping, and how to avoid fragile system-to-system links with no operational safety.

hero.title: API integration is more than connecting two endpoints
hero.subtitle: It is about keeping data, state, and responsibility aligned across systems with different behaviour and reliability.

intro: A good integration is not defined by endpoint count. It is defined by reliable data flow and manageable exceptions.
In business workflows, validation, retries, monitoring, and visibility into failure states matter as much as the integration call itself.

sections.title: Where this technology is a strong fit
body: Technology should fit the product and delivery reality, not just developer preference.
bullets: connecting ERP, CRM, ecommerce, and internal tools

sections.title: What to be careful about
body: A technology’s strengths disappear quickly when it is used in the wrong context or without a sound architecture.
bullets: direct links with no control or monitoring

sections.title: Typical fit for a business project
body: The choice should support delivery speed, maintainability, and operational confidence over time.
bullets: portals and internal tools with external dependencies

faq.question: Is API documentation enough for a successful integration project?
faq.answer: No. The process, the data model, failure states, and operational context matter just as much.

faq.question: Can integrations be delivered in stages?
faq.answer: Yes. That is usually safer and more practical than a single large integration jump.

faq.question: What if one of the systems is awkward to integrate with?
faq.answer: That is common. It often means adjusting the design, adding a supporting layer, or making workflow changes around the limitation.

for: portals and internal tools with external dependencies
notFor: direct links with no control or monitoring
